issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name on button
issuesFound.explanation: The button elements with no accessible name (no text, aria-label, or aria-labelledby) are missing proper labeling. Screen reader users will announce these buttons as "button" without context.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 103: `<button type="button" class="nav-toggle">` → `<button type="button" class="nav-toggle" aria-label="Menu toggle">` (already has aria-hidden="true" span, but no accessible name)
  Line 105: `<button type="button">Solutions</button>` → `<button type="button" aria-label="Solutions">Solutions</button>`
  Line 107: `<button type="button" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="resources-submenu" aria-haspopup="true">Resources</button>` → `<button type="button" aria-label="Resources dropdown">Resources</button>`
  Line 109: `<button type="button" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="solutions-submenu" aria-haspopup="true">Solutions</button>` → `<button type="button" aria-label="Solutions dropdown">Solutions</button>`
  Line 111: `<button type="button" class="nav-toggle">` → `<button type="button" class="nav-toggle" aria-label="Menu toggle">` (already has aria-hidden="true" span, but no accessible name)

issuesFound.title: Missing alt attribute on image
issuesFound.explanation: Multiple elements lack alt attributes, making them inaccessible to screen readers. Users relying on screen readers cannot understand the purpose of these images.
issuesFound.fix: Line 100: `` → `` Line 122: `

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name on link
issuesFound.explanation: The <a> element for "Luminary Studio — go to homepage" has no visible text content, aria-label, or aria-labelledby attributes. This means screen reader users will not know what this link does.
issuesFound.fix: <a href="/" aria-label="Luminary Studio — go to homepage">

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name on button
issuesFound.explanation: The <button> element for "Open navigation menu" has no visible text content, aria-label, or aria-labelledby attributes. This makes it difficult for screen reader users to understand the purpose of this button.
issuesFound.fix: <button type="button" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="nav-menu" aria-label="Open navigation menu">

issuesFound.title: Table header missing scope
issuesFound.explanation: The <th> element inside the <nav> element has no scope attribute. This is semantically incorrect and can cause issues with screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: <th id="nav-menu" scope="row">
